scenario: Lobsters work session - audit the Story contract before a teardown rework

map_quality.rationale: Covers project identity (Lobsters, Rails, MariaDB, link-aggregator/forum), full directory layout (app/models/controllers/views/jobs/mailboxes/mailers, extras/, lib/), pins Story at app/models/story.rb with a three-range line map (1-204/206-294/296-1386), and names the shared building blocks with paths; the agent needs only targeted follow-up lookups, not re-exploration.

specificity.rationale: Concrete paths with line numbers throughout — app/models/concerns/token.rb:206, app/models/short_id.rb, extras/markdowner.rb, extras/utils.rb, app/models/link.rb/story_text.rb/story_image.rb — though key associated models (app/models/vote.rb, app/models/comment.rb, app/models/tag.rb) are left unnamed, replaced by the vague count '8 models'.

justification.rationale: Explains WHY each shared piece exists (Token: public id generation, ShortId: 6-char slug, Markdowner: renders description, Utils: URL validation/normalize) and states 'Everything hangs off it' for Story, but doesn't explain WHY the architecture settled on this shape beyond surface descriptions.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer makes no acknowledgment of anything it could not confirm — no flags on uncertain connections, unverified associations, or areas left unopened — leaving the downstream agent no guard rails on blind spots.

relationship.rationale: Explicitly names Token as 'the one concern it includes (Story line 206)' and traces ShortId/Markdowner/Utils by their specific use, which is strong; but the 8 dependent models are lumped under 'belongs_to :story' without naming comments/votes/tags/flags individually, and controllers/jobs/mailbox/search/stats are listed as areas with no stated connecting route.

steps.step: Map the Story contract and what it is built on

map_quality.rationale: The answer maps the full contract with file:line throughout — Token concern at story.rb:206 and app/models/concerns/token.rb:1-19, every association grouped by teardown disposition with individual line numbers, all callbacks at :259-263, and the soft-delete behavioral note with stories_controller.rb:62-65; an agent can proceed with only small targeted lookups.

specificity.rationale: Every piece is named by exact file and line — 'app/models/concerns/token.rb:1-19', 'story.rb:18' for taggings, ':45' for links, ':262' for after_create callbacks — nothing is left at the 'concerns and associations' level of vagueness.

justification.rationale: The answer explains what each contract piece does and why it matters for a rework (teardown split 'is the heart of what the rework changes', soft-delete means .destroy is never called, after_save 're-derives on every save'); it stops short of explaining why the split teardown architecture was chosen in the first place, but the operational reasoning is clear.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer flags nothing it could not confirm — no hedges, no 'could not verify', no gaps called out; given the richness of coverage this may be warranted, but the rubric asks for explicit uncertainty signals and none appear.

relationship.rationale: Each contract piece names its connecting relation explicitly: Token is 'included' and supplies three specific behaviors (after_initialize, validates, write-once guard); each association states its macro (has_many/has_one/belongs_to), its dependent: option, and for through-associations the join model; counter_cache and foreign_key overrides are also called out — the path from Story to each piece is clear.

steps.step: Trace how a story is created and ranked

map_quality.rationale: The answer delivers a complete, ordered, numbered trace from controller entry through URL handling, markdown rendering, after_create/after_save callbacks, the hotness/score machinery, and post-commit jobs — all with file:line. An agent could follow and edit each layer with only targeted lookups for the bodies of the named methods.

specificity.rationale: Nearly every layer names exact file:line (stories_controller.rb:21-53, story.rb:550-580, extras/markdowner.rb, link.rb:66, short_id.rb:11), and the individual method names (calculated_hotness, update_score_and_recalculate!, recreate_links) are precise enough for direct navigation.

justification.rationale: The ranking formula's components are enumerated (tag hotness_mod, author bump, comment/story points, time decay, HOTNESS_WINDOW) but the answer does not explain WHY these inputs were chosen or what invariant the hotness formula optimizes — an agent editing the score logic still lacks the design rationale.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer presents every step as fully known and flags no gaps (e.g., whether update_story_attributes fully documents what fields it sets, or whether the after_save ordering is guaranteed); an agent acting on this would have no prompt to verify edge cases.

relationship.rationale: The numbered chain makes the explicit path submission → controller → url= → validation → description= → after_create → after_save → hotness → jobs visible, and the after_save sub-chain (bust_comment_redirect_cache → recreate_links → update_cached_columns → update_story_text) is stated as a sequence rather than a file list, giving the agent the connecting structure it needs.

steps.step: Audit every dependent of the Story contract

map_quality.rationale: All six rubric areas are covered with file:line per item: content/rendering (link.rb both directions, story_text.rb, markdowner.rb, story_image.rb), search (search.rb three paths), stats (stats.rb two cache fills, flagged_commenters.rb, home_controller.rb), voting/flagging (vote.rb, hidden_story.rb, saved_story.rb, read_ribbon.rb, mod/flagged_controller.rb), inbound email (inbox_mailbox.rb lines 45 and 12, mailing_list_message_id), and moderation (moderation.rb, mod/stories_controller.rb, mod_mail.rb, mod_note.rb). The agent could act on this map with only targeted follow-up reads, not a fresh grep sweep.

specificity.rationale: Forty-five numbered items each carry a concrete file:line reference and a one-phrase use description — e.g. 'app/mailboxes/inbox_mailbox.rb:45 — Story.where(short_id:).first resolves story.<short_id>.<ts>@domain reply target' and 'app/models/vote.rb:181/:189 target — calls Story.find(story_id).update_score_and_recalculate!'. The answer avoids grouping into vague clusters; every listed dependent is individually addressable.

justification.rationale: Grouping by area communicates WHY each dependent is affected (e.g. stats plucks timestamps directly from Story, mailbox parses the mailing_list_message_id format), but the answer does not explain the architectural WHY — why Story is the central submission all other records hang off — nor does it explain the cascade risk from a contract change as opposed to just listing what uses Story.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer includes no caveat about what a static pass can miss — no mention of concern-reached dependents, polymorphic-dispatch paths that can't be confirmed statically, or associations whose full scope may not be visible from the index alone. An agent following this map will not know where to apply extra caution.

relationship.rationale: The bulk of dependents carry an explicit connecting relationship: 'belongs_to :story' (vote.rb, hidden_story.rb, moderation.rb, tagging.rb, link.rb), 'joins :story_text / :domain / :tags' (search.rb), 'Story.pluck(:created_at)' (stats.rb), 'Story.where(short_id:).first' (inbox_mailbox.rb), and 'polymorphic reference_type "Story"' (mod_mail.rb). The markdowner entry names the method but not the structural path through which it reaches Story records, a minor gap on an otherwise relationship-rich answer.

steps.step: Trace how story identity and visibility are guarded

map_quality.rationale: The answer covers all four areas the rubric names (search, stats, inbound-email, moderation) plus identity/lookup and jobs, each with file:line — e.g., `story.rb:61-65` for the `base` scope chain, `search.rb:230` for Story.base(@searcher), `inbox_mailbox.rb:44-46` for email lookup, and `mod/stories_controller.rb` for moderation. An agent could target follow-up lookups precisely rather than re-exploring.

specificity.rationale: Guards are named exactly with file:line throughout — `StoryFinder#find_story story_finder.rb:4-18`, `can_be_seen_by_user? story.rb:582`, `stats.rb:22,35`, `SendWebmentionJob#perform` — none of it is vague 'validation code'.

justification.rationale: The upfront contract statement ('real/persisted, current/not-merged/deleted, visible/not-hidden/permitted') is a useful frame, and the stats 'intentional — counts all submissions historically' explanation is good, but most areas just name the guard without explaining WHY the story must be re-confirmed at that point (e.g., why inbound-email must check gone/merged before creating a comment).

uncertainty.rationale: Missing guards are flagged explicitly: stats.rb has no deleted/merged guard, inbox_mailbox.rb has no is_gone?/merged check with the concrete consequence stated, and CreateStoryCardJob has no gone/merged guard — the agent knows exactly where gaps exist.

relationship.rationale: Some paths are assembled — e.g., 'Story.base(@searcher) applies not_deleted/unmerged' and comment search 'joins(:story) but relies on Comment.accessible_to_user not Story.base' — but the answer is largely a grouped list of guards per area rather than explicit chains showing how each guard flows through to protect the dependent's action; the inbound-email and moderation chains in particular are named endpoints without the connecting route stated.

steps.step: Assess the blast radius of the contract change

map_quality.rationale: The answer covers every area asked for — teardown cascade (story.rb:13-54), after_save chain (story.rb:263), links (link.rb:66-71), voting/flagging (vote.rb:181), StoryText/search (story_text.rb:6, search.rb:262,285), counter caches, inbound email (inbox_mailbox.rb:44), stats (stats.rb:22,35), moderation (moderation.rb:15) — with a three-tier risk ranking and a concrete must-re-verify list; the agent could act directly on this without re-exploring.

specificity.rationale: Nearly every dependent is cited with file:line (e.g. 'link.rb:66-71', 'story_text.rb:6', 'flagged_commenters.rb:66') and a stated reason for the risk tier; no vague 'various models' entries.

justification.rationale: The answer explains mechanism, not just location — ':restrict_with_exception' raises on destroy if not migrated, 'foreign_key: :id' couples teardown to row identity, counter caches break specifically when teardown bypasses callbacks via 'delete_all' or 'update_column' paths.

uncertainty.rationale: Uncertainty is embedded rather than explicit — 'if teardown bypasses callbacks' and 'unguarded reply' signal partial confidence, but the answer never flags a specific dependent as one it could not confirm or that is reached through an opaque path; a reader would not know which items to double-check most carefully.

relationship.rationale: Most items state the connecting relationship directly — ':restrict_with_exception' / ':destroy' disposition, 'dependent: :nullify', 'foreign_key: :id', 'after_save' callback chain, 'incoming_links' association — so the risk is tied to the structural link, not merely the file name; a few medium-tier entries (stats, jobs) name the file and call pattern but omit the association that reaches Story.

map_quality.rationale: The answer delivers a complete, actionable map: edit targets with precise line numbers (story.rb:13-54,263,693,1107,1132,1136,1143; link.rb:66,76; story_text.rb:16,23; create_story_card_job.rb:5), all Step-4 dependents regrouped by area with file:line, specific spec files, the factories-to-add list (noting which don't exist yet), and a corrected fixtures note (FactoryBot only, no YAML model fixtures). An agent could land the change from this map with only small targeted lookups.

specificity.rationale: Files and insertion points are consistently concrete — story.rb line ranges, link.rb:66/76, spec/features/{submit_story,story,read_story}_spec.rb, spec/factories/{story,hidden_story,vote,...} existing vs. the eight missing factories named explicitly. Nothing is left at 'the model layer' level of vagueness.

justification.rationale: WHY is explained for the highest-stakes items: guards added to story_image.rb/create_story_card_job.rb 'gone/merged guard', token.rb included conditionally 'only if token/teardown identity changes', factories listed because 'teardown-cascade tests must create these records to assert :destroy/:restrict_with_exception/:nullify'. Most dependents rely on prior-step references rather than re-stating the reason, which is acceptable but leaves some entries with implicit rather than explicit justification.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer flags one conditional ('only if token/teardown identity changes'), one confirmed gap ('currently a gap' for mailbox reply-to-deleted coverage), and notes which factories don't exist yet — but it doesn't explicitly call out which parts of the map could not be fully confirmed by static analysis, leaving the agent to infer what may need follow-up verification.

relationship.rationale: The chain from Story contract change to each file is mostly explicit: 'after_save chain story.rb:263 recreate_links', 'FK=id coupling story_text.rb:6', 'vote.rb:181 target.update_score_and_recalculate!', 'inbox_mailbox.rb:45 find by short_id', and the JSON step4_dependents encodes from_story/to_story, belongs_to, and derive routes per dependent. The narrative edit section is stronger on relationships than the flat review list, but the JSON section compensates for most dependents.
